title: hieracium pseudodollineri subsp. pseudodollineri
intro: Hieracium pseudodollineri subsp. pseudodollineri, also known as Dolliner's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Dolliner's Hawkweed is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
